name: Warden-Sentry
physical: Seven-foot humanoid silhouette of compressed white plasma, outline flickering like a candle in a glass box. Forearms taper into angular hard-light blade projections glowing cyan at the cutting edge. Ground scorches black beneath every footfall. Materializes from Gate-Herald transit in flashes of searing light.
narrative: Advance in perfect geometric formations, cutting through infantry with surgical hard-light swipes. When one falls, the 8m plasma burst forces a tactical choice: cluster to maintain firepower and risk chain reactions, or spread thin and be picked apart individually. Leaves a scorched patch burning 5 seconds.

name: Obelisk-Drone
physical: Hovering diamond the size of a car, rotating slowly on its vertical axis, emitting a subsonic hum felt in the chest. Projects a Null-Frequency Field — a 30m zone where energy weapons cease to function. Plasma dissipates. Lasers scatter into incoherence. Shields overload and collapse. Enemies must fight with physical weapons only.
narrative: Strips technological advantages from every faction that relies on energy doctrine — which is most of them. Combat inside the field becomes a primitive brawl where Guardian hard-light blades dominate. On death: Null-Frequency inverts — blinding EMP shorts all electronics within 15m for 4 seconds.

name: Prism-Strider
physical: Quadrupedal crystalline energy construct, twelve feet tall. Legs: sharp elongated hard-light triangles. Torso: rotating hexagonal prism housing a concentrated plasma core. Convergence Beam — sustained white-hot plasma lance sweeping like a searchlight from hell. Tanks don't explode under its fire — armor softens, droops, and deforms as molecular bonds fail.
narrative: 25m death radius flips light vehicles. The chain-reaction risk with adjacent Striders is HIGH — tight formations are devastating firepower platforms but a single kill can cascade catastrophically through a grouped position.

name: Tesseract-Ward
physical: Massive slowly rotating cube of interlocking hard-light panels, hovering three feet above ground. Projects Lattice-Shield — a dome of interlocking hexagons absorbing kinetic impacts. Each absorbed shell sends cyan light ripples across the surface. Shield holds until overwhelmed — then shatters violently into razor-sharp light-fragments.
narrative: Cannot move while projecting. No offensive capability. But forces advancing behind it are protected while attackers are forced to reposition. 35m death combines lattice shatter (shrapnel) AND core detonation — the most dangerous Guardian ground-unit death in the game.

name: Nexus-Colossus
guardianNote: 200m coordination aura. Killing it freezes all nearby Guardians for 2–3 seconds — brief window to press advantage. CATASTROPHIC death: 50m+ stellar core collapse, guaranteed chain-reaction with any Guardian in range. Attack must be conducted spread wide or accept total losses.
physical: Three-story central spire of blinding white energy on four angled hard-light buttress-legs. Apex: pulsating sphere of contained stellar plasma — desiccates organic matter within 50m passively. Judgment Beam bores through fortifications, melts bunkers to glass, fills trenches with slag.
narrative: 50m+ CATASTROPHIC death radius: stellar core undergoes uncontained fusion collapse, vaporizing everything in range. GUARANTEED chain-reaction with any Guardian unit nearby. Assaulting requires spreading formation wide or accepting total losses as the price of victory.

name: Arc-Lance
physical: Geometric arrowhead of cyan energy capable of instantaneous right-angle turns that would pulverize any biological pilot. Fires Disruption Needles — pencil-thin beams piercing cockpit glass and detonating munitions inside the target. Rams shielded targets at full speed when weapons are insufficient. On death: fragments into dozens of razor-sharp energy splinters shredding nearby aircraft.
narrative: Physics-defying maneuvers. Conventional tracking is useless — targeting systems designed for aircraft following ballistic arcs have no answer for instantaneous 90-degree direction changes at full velocity.

name: Shroud-Veil
physical: Flat disc bending light around its hull — invisible from below and from standard sensors. Drops Containment Spheres that embed in the ground and bloom into columns of hard-light impaling anything above. Columns persist 8–10 seconds — a forest of lethal light-pillars denying entire terrain zones.
narrative: On death: light-bending field inverts — blinding flash visible for miles, temporarily rendering all optical sensors within 500m non-functional. The area around a dead Shroud-Veil is temporarily blind.

name: Chorus-Resonator
physical: Three interlocking rings rotating on different axes with a vibrating energy sphere at the center. Broadcasts Frequency Cascade — tuned harmonics that crack materials in sequence: titanium, glass, ceramic, bone. Causes violent nausea, vision blur, and progressive motor control loss in biological units within range.
narrative: On death: frequency spikes to a devastating peak, shattering everything brittle within 40m — helmet visors, viewport glass, and bones. The death radius is specifically catastrophic for unshielded biological troops.

name: Gate-Herald
physical: Floating hexagonal frame twenty meters wide — a mobile teleportation gate. Guardian forces walk through from orbit, arriving in the combat zone instantly combat-ready. A single Gate-Herald deploys an entire assault force in 30 seconds. Destroying one mid-transit bifurcates the force being transported.
narrative: Death implosion: localized gravitational anomaly pulls everything within 30m toward the former gate position, then detonates outward. HIGH chain-reaction risk — Gate-Heralds frequently operate near other Guardian units they were just transporting.

name: Corona-Sentinel
guardianNote: CATASTROPHIC death. 100m radius scorched. Single most destructive airborne death event in the game. Must be engaged from extreme range with maximum force concentration — drawn-out engagements guarantee catastrophic losses.
physical: Fifty-meter ring housing a miniature captive star. Rain evaporates before reaching it. Snow sublimates within a kilometer. Solar Lances — focused beams from the captive star — combine scalpel precision with meteor-scale kinetic force. Each impact creates a 10m pool of molten glass.
narrative: On death: captive star undergoes brief uncontained fusion collapse — 100m radius scorched. THE single most destructive airborne death event in the game. Engage from extreme range with maximum concentrated fire and no hesitation.

name: Spark-Mote
guardianNote: Hidden formation controller: if identified and destroyed, ALL remaining Motes detonate simultaneously. Massive area damage. A calculated high-risk play.
physical: Hundreds of tiny energy packets barely larger than conventional torpedoes, operating in perfectly synchronized geometric formations. Each Mote detonates on contact, burning through hull plating. Impossible to shoot individually — requires area-effect weapons to engage the swarm.
narrative: Destroying the hidden formation controller causes ALL remaining Motes to detonate simultaneously. A calculated risk — accept some hull damage from the detonation to eliminate the entire swarm in one decisive action.

name: Aegis-Monolith
physical: Massive rectangular slab of hard-light — a floating wall hundreds of meters tall. Barrier Lattice of interlocking hexagons physically blocks all incoming fire. Energy beams are absorbed and fed back into the core to strengthen the lattice. The only way through: overwhelming concentrated fire on a single point until the lattice fails.
narrative: On death: lattice shatters into millions of shards spraying outward at relativistic velocity — hull damage to every ship within 2 kilometers. Fleets that kill Aegis-Monoliths without repositioning first pay heavily for the kill.

name: Verdict-Cruiser
physical: Elongated hexagonal prism, 300m long. Six facet-lines along the hull open to reveal the plasma core within. Fires six Judgment Lances simultaneously — sustained beams that sublimate enemy hulls (solid matter to gas, bypassing the liquid phase). Most dangerous at broadside angle when all six lances fire in parallel.
narrative: On death: plasma erupts through all six facets simultaneously — a six-pointed star of destruction extending 500m in each direction. High-chain risk with any Guardian escort in the vicinity.

name: Warden-Carrier
guardianNote: CATASTROPHIC death. Internal gate collapse (implosion) followed immediately by ALL docked Spark-Motes detonating simultaneously. Visible from planetary surfaces. Must be engaged at extreme range with full fleet spread.
physical: Dyson sphere fragment with hundreds of hexagonal bays housing Spark-Motes and Arc-Lances. Central Gate-Herald frame deploys Guardian ground forces directly to planetary surfaces, bypassing the orbital battle entirely. Destroying it stops Mote replenishment, grounds interceptors, and severs the ground-force deployment gate.
narrative: On death: internal gate collapses (implosion) followed by ALL docked Spark-Motes detonating simultaneously. Visible from planetary surfaces. CATASTROPHIC. The most dangerous Guardian ship to kill outside of the Celestial-Throne itself.

name: Celestial-Throne
guardianNote: THE NEXUS OBJECTIVE. Win condition: Conquer The Nexus (sys-nexus) → all Vorax nodes purged → VICTORY. The Celestial-Throne IS both the conquest objective and the Unification negotiation partner. Only one exists. Its destruction: VD radius 5km, TOTAL DESTRUCTION of everything in range — both sides lose. This outcome closes the Unification path permanently.
physical: Perfect icosahedron, one kilometer across. Each of 20 faces a reconfigurable hard-light panel. Center: fragment of the Celestial Core — the technology capable of stopping the Vorax. Moves by displacing space itself. Resonance Purge: opens all faces, exposing the Core fragment, and the target ship does not explode or burn — it comes APART. Bolts unscrewing. Hull plates separating. Crew compartments opening to vacuum. 8 seconds. No defense.
narrative: There is only ONE. Destroying it is functionally impossible through direct assault — VD radius 5km annihilates everything nearby, both sides. The Celestial-Throne IS the final boss of Conquest. IS the negotiation partner of Unification. The game's ultimate challenge: an ancient immortal intelligence in an invincible geometric fortress, guarding the only weapon that can save the galaxy, and refusing to let anyone use it.
